com.jsyn.util

Class VoiceAllocator

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.jsyn.util.VoiceAllocator
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Instrument

    public class VoiceAllocator
extends java.lang.Object
implements Instrument
    Allocate voices based on an integer tag. The tag could, for example, be a MIDI note number. Or a tag could be an int that always increments. Use the same tag to refer to a voice for noteOn() and noteOff(). If no new voices are available then a voice in use will be stolen.
    Author:
    Phil Burk (C) 2011 Mobileer Inc

      Create an allocator for the array of UnitVoices.

    • Constructor Detail

      • VoiceAllocator

        public VoiceAllocator(UnitVoice[] voices)
        Create an allocator for the array of UnitVoices. The array must be full of instantiated UnitVoices that are connected to some kind of mixer.
        Parameters:
        voices -

    • Method Detail

      • getSynthesizer

        public Synthesizer getSynthesizer()

      • getVoiceCount

        public int getVoiceCount()
        Returns:
        number of UnitVoices passed to the allocator.

      • allocate

        protected UnitVoice allocate(int tag)
        Allocate a Voice associated with this tag. It will first pick a voice already assigned to that tag. Next it will pick the oldest voice that is off. Next it will pick the oldest voice that is on. If you are using timestamps to play the voice in the future then you should use the noteOn() noteOff() and setPort() methods.
        Parameters:
        tag -
        Returns:
        Voice that is most available.

      • isOn

        protected boolean isOn(int tag)

      • off

        protected UnitVoice off(int tag)

      • allNotesOff

        public void allNotesOff(TimeStamp timeStamp)
        Turn off all the note currently on.
        Specified by:
        allNotesOff in interface Instrument

      • noteOn

        public void noteOn(int tag,
                   double frequency,
                   double amplitude,
                   TimeStamp timeStamp)
        Play a note on the voice and associate it with the given tag. if needed a new voice will be allocated and an old voice may be turned off.
        Specified by:
        noteOn in interface Instrument

      • noteOn

        public void noteOn(int tag,
                   double frequency,
                   double amplitude,
                   VoiceOperation operation,
                   TimeStamp timeStamp)
        Play a note on the voice and associate it with the given tag. if needed a new voice will be allocated and an old voice may be turned off. Apply an operation to the voice.

      • noteOff

        public void noteOff(int tag,
                    TimeStamp timeStamp)
        Turn off the voice associated with the given tag if allocated.
        Specified by:
        noteOff in interface Instrument

                    java.lang.String portName,
                    double value,
                    TimeStamp timeStamp)
        Set a port on the voice associated with the given tag if allocated.
        Specified by:
        setPort in interface Instrument
